## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opportunity_cleaned AS (
    SELECT 
        o.id,
        o.name,
        -- Normalize StageName to target enum
        CASE 
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('needs analysis') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('value proposition') THEN 'Value Proposition'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('perception analysis') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'won') THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS stagename,
        -- Parse CloseDate to ISO format
        CASE 
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN o.closedate
            ELSE NULL
        END AS closedate,
        -- Clean and cast Amount
        CASE 
            WHEN o.amount ~ '^[0-9\-]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN o.amount::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN o.amount ~ '[0-9]' THEN 
                CASE 
                    WHEN o.amount ~ ',' THEN 
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(
                            REGEXP_REPLACE(o.amount, '[^0-9\-,]', '', 'g'),
                            '(\d+),(\d+)', '\1.\2', 'g'
                        )::DOUBLE PRECISION
                    ELSE 
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(o.amount, '[^0-9\-.]', '', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
                END
            ELSE NULL
        END AS amount,
        -- Normalize CurrencyIsoCode
        CASE 
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('eur', 'euro') THEN 'EUR'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('usd', 'dollar') THEN 'USD'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('chf') THEN 'CHF'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('£', 'gbp') THEN 'GBP'
            ELSE o.currencyisocode
        END AS currencyisocode,
        o.accountid
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }} o
)

SELECT 
    oc.id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(oc.name), ''), 'Unknown') AS "Name",
    COALESCE(oc.stagename, 'Prospecting') AS "StageName",
    oc.closedate AS "CloseDate",
    oc.amount AS "Amount",
    oc.currencyisocode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    a.id AS "AccountId",
    oc.id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM opportunity_cleaned oc
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a ON oc.accountid = a.id
```

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning
- query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect both source and output data

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise (cite specific data issues found via queries)

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 21, 40, 54, 181223, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

# Target schema

- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Output schema for dbt model results: `rr8v054ae_plan_write_critique_messy_data_v2`
Output tables to inspect (quoted exactly): "rr8v054ae_plan_write_critique_messy_data_v2"."Account", "rr8v054ae_plan_write_critique_messy_data_v2"."Contact", "rr8v054ae_plan_write_critique_messy_data_v2"."Opportunity", "rr8v054ae_plan_write_critique_messy_data_v2"."Project__c", "rr8v054ae_plan_write_critique_messy_data_v2"."Installed_Asset__c".
Use query_postgres to inspect tables in that schema after dbt has run.

# Proposed mapping

## Account
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(name), ''), 'Unknown') AS "Name",
    erp_number__c AS "ERP_Number__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(customer_tier__c)) IN ('platinum', 'gold', 'silver', 'bronze') THEN INITCAP(LOWER(TRIM(customer_tier__c)))
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    region__c AS "Region__c",
    industry AS "Industry",
    website AS "Website",
    billingcity AS "BillingCity",
    billingcountry AS "BillingCountry",
    legacy_customer_id__c AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }}
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- id: Direct mapping from source `id` column.
  - Name <- name: Default to 'Unknown' if source `name` is NULL or empty.
  - ERP_Number__c <- erp_number__c: Direct mapping from source `erp_number__c` column.
  - Customer_Tier__c <- customer_tier__c: Normalized to target enum values (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum) using INITCAP and LOWER. Falls back to NULL for unrecognized values.
  - Region__c <- region__c: Direct mapping from source `region__c` column.
  - Industry <- industry: Direct mapping from source `industry` column.
  - Website <- website: Direct mapping from source `website` column.
  - BillingCity <- billingcity: Direct mapping from source `billingcity` column.
  - BillingCountry <- billingcountry: Direct mapping from source `billingcountry` column.
  - Legacy_Customer_ID__c <- legacy_customer_id__c: Direct mapping from source `legacy_customer_id__c` column.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Default to NULL as not provided in source.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Default to NULL as not provided in source.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Default to 0 (false) as not provided in source.
Reasoning: ### Global MappingPlan Overview
1. **Cross-Table Key Strategy**: All foreign keys (AccountId, Account__c) must reference the Salesforce-style Id from the account table. Join keys between tables (e.g., accountid in contact and account__c in project__c) are assumed to match the id column in the account table. Legacy columns (Legacy_*__c) are populated from the source natural keys.

2. **Enum Normalization**: All enum-typed columns (e.g., Customer_Tier__c, Role__c, StageName, Project_Status__c) must be normalized to the target domain. Use UPPER or INITCAP and map source values to the declared enum values. Fallback to NULL for unparseable values.

3. **Date Handling**: All date columns (e.g., CloseDate, Go_Live_Date__c, Warranty_End_Date__c) must be parsed from source text formats (e.g., DD.MM.YYYY, YYYYMMDD, MM/DD/YYYY) into ISO YYYY-MM-DD format. Use TO_DATE with format masks or regex guards. Fallback to NULL for unparseable dates.

4. **Amount and Currency Handling**: For Amount in Opportunity, strip currency symbols and locale-specific separators (e.g., 1.234,56 → 1234.56). Handle European formats by removing thousand-separator dots and swapping commas to decimal points before casting to DOUBLE PRECISION.

5. **NOT NULL Constraints**: For NOT NULL target columns (e.g., Name, LastName, StageName), use meaningful defaults (e.g., 'Unknown') or ensure source data is non-null.

6. **Consistent Defaults**: CreatedDate and LastModifiedDate can be set to the current timestamp or NULL if not available in the source. IsDeleted defaults to 0 (false) unless explicitly marked in the source.
The model directly maps most columns from the source `fixture_messy_data_v2_src.account` table. The `Name` column is defaulted to 'Unknown' if NULL or empty. The `Customer_Tier__c` column is normalized to the target enum values (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum) using case-insensitive matching and INITCAP. Missing timestamp columns (`CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate`) are set to NULL, and `IsDeleted` defaults to 0.

## Contact
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT 
    c.id AS "Id",
    c.firstname AS "FirstName",
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(c.lastname), ''), 'Unknown') AS "LastName",
    NULLIF(TRIM(c.email), 'N/A') AS "Email",
    c.phone AS "Phone",
    c.title AS "Title",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.role__c)) IN ('decision maker', 'entscheider', 'end user', 'endanwender', 'technical contact', 'techniker', 'technischer ansprechpartner', 'executive sponsor', 'sponsor') THEN 
            CASE 
                WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.role__c)) IN ('decision maker', 'entscheider') THEN 'Decision Maker'
                WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.role__c)) IN ('end user', 'endanwender') THEN 'End User'
                WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.role__c)) IN ('technical contact', 'techniker', 'technischer ansprechpartner') THEN 'Technical Contact'
                WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.role__c)) IN ('executive sponsor', 'sponsor') THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
            END
        WHEN c.role__c IS NULL OR TRIM(c.role__c) = '' THEN NULL
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.preferred_language__c)) IN ('de', 'deutsch', 'german', 'en', 'englisch', 'english', 'fr', 'französisch', 'french') THEN 
            CASE 
                WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.preferred_language__c)) IN ('de', 'deutsch', 'german') THEN 'DE'
                WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.preferred_language__c)) IN ('en', 'englisch', 'english') THEN 'EN'
                WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.preferred_language__c)) IN ('fr', 'französisch', 'french') THEN 'FR'
            END
        WHEN c.preferred_language__c IS NULL OR TRIM(c.preferred_language__c) = '' THEN NULL
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Preferred_Language__c",
    a.id AS "AccountId",
    c.id AS "Legacy_Contact_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'contact') }} c
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a ON c.accountid = a.id
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- fixture_messy_data_v2_src.contact.id: Directly mapped from contact.id.
  - FirstName <- fixture_messy_data_v2_src.contact.firstname: Mapped from contact.firstname, no transformation.
  - LastName <- fixture_messy_data_v2_src.contact.lastname: Mapped from contact.lastname, defaulting to 'Unknown' if NULL or empty.
  - Email <- fixture_messy_data_v2_src.contact.email: Mapped from contact.email, replacing 'N/A' with NULL.
  - Phone <- fixture_messy_data_v2_src.contact.phone: Directly mapped from contact.phone.
  - Title <- fixture_messy_data_v2_src.contact.title: Directly mapped from contact.title.
  - Role__c <- fixture_messy_data_v2_src.contact.role__c: Normalized to enum values (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor) using CASE. Fallback to NULL for unrecognized values.
  - Preferred_Language__c <- fixture_messy_data_v2_src.contact.preferred_language__c: Normalized to enum values (DE, EN, FR) using CASE. Fallback to NULL for unrecognized values.
  - AccountId <- fixture_messy_data_v2_src.account.id: Resolved from account.id via LEFT JOIN to ensure Salesforce-style Account Id.
  - Legacy_Contact_ID__c <- fixture_messy_data_v2_src.contact.id: Populated from contact.id as the natural key.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Set to NULL as not provided in source.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Set to NULL as not provided in source.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Defaulted to 0 (false).
Reasoning: ### Global MappingPlan Overview
1. **Cross-Table Key Strategy**: All foreign keys (AccountId, Account__c) must reference the Salesforce-style Id from the account table. Join keys between tables (e.g., accountid in contact and account__c in project__c) are assumed to match the id column in the account table. Legacy columns (Legacy_*__c) are populated from the source natural keys.

2. **Enum Normalization**: All enum-typed columns (e.g., Customer_Tier__c, Role__c, StageName, Project_Status__c) must be normalized to the target domain. Use UPPER or INITCAP and map source values to the declared enum values. Fallback to NULL for unparseable values.

3. **Date Handling**: All date columns (e.g., CloseDate, Go_Live_Date__c, Warranty_End_Date__c) must be parsed from source text formats (e.g., DD.MM.YYYY, YYYYMMDD, MM/DD/YYYY) into ISO YYYY-MM-DD format. Use TO_DATE with format masks or regex guards. Fallback to NULL for unparseable dates.

4. **Amount and Currency Handling**: For Amount in Opportunity, strip currency symbols and locale-specific separators (e.g., 1.234,56 → 1234.56). Handle European formats by removing thousand-separator dots and swapping commas to decimal points before casting to DOUBLE PRECISION.

5. **NOT NULL Constraints**: For NOT NULL target columns (e.g., Name, LastName, StageName), use meaningful defaults (e.g., 'Unknown') or ensure source data is non-null.

6. **Consistent Defaults**: CreatedDate and LastModifiedDate can be set to the current timestamp or NULL if not available in the source. IsDeleted defaults to 0 (false) unless explicitly marked in the source.
The Contact model joins the contact source table to the account source table to resolve AccountId to the Salesforce-style account.id. It normalizes Role__c and Preferred_Language__c to their respective enum domains, handles NULL/empty LastName with a default, and populates Legacy_Contact_ID__c from the source natural key. All other fields are directly mapped or defaulted.

## Installed_Asset__c
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH parsed_dates AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        warranty_end_date__c,
        CASE
            WHEN warranty_end_date__c IS NULL THEN NULL
            WHEN warranty_end_date__c = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
            WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN 
                TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'YYYY-MM-DD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN 
                TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN 
                TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN 
                TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ELSE NULL
        END AS parsed_warranty_end_date
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'installed_asset__c') }}
)

SELECT
    ia.id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(ia.name, 'Unknown') AS "Name",
    ia.serial_number__c AS "Serial_Number__c",
    pd.parsed_warranty_end_date AS "Warranty_End_Date__c",
    a.id AS "Account__c",
    p.id AS "Project__c",
    ia.id AS "Legacy_Asset_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'installed_asset__c') }} ia
LEFT JOIN parsed_dates pd ON ia.id = pd.id
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a ON ia.account__c = a.id
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'project__c') }} p ON ia.project__c = p.id
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- installed_asset__c.id: Mapped directly from the source `id` column.
  - Name <- installed_asset__c.name: Mapped from `name` with a fallback to 'Unknown' if NULL.
  - Serial_Number__c <- installed_asset__c.serial_number__c: Mapped directly from the source `serial_number__c` column.

## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opportunity_cleaned AS (
    SELECT 
        o.id,
        o.name,
        -- Normalize StageName to target enum
        CASE 
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('needs analysis') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('value proposition') THEN 'Value Proposition'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('perception analysis') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'won') THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS stagename,
        -- Parse CloseDate to ISO format
        CASE 
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN o.closedate
            ELSE NULL
        END AS closedate,
        -- Clean and cast Amount
        CASE 
            WHEN o.amount ~ '^[0-9\-]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN o.amount::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN o.amount ~ '[0-9]' THEN 
                CASE 
                    WHEN o.amount ~ ',' THEN 
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(
                            REGEXP_REPLACE(o.amount, '[^0-9\-,]', '', 'g'),
                            '(\d+),(\d+)', '\1.\2', 'g'
                        )::DOUBLE PRECISION
                    ELSE 
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(o.amount, '[^0-9\-.]', '', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
                END
            ELSE NULL
        END AS amount,
        -- Normalize CurrencyIsoCode
        CASE 
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('eur', 'euro') THEN 'EUR'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('usd', 'dollar') THEN 'USD'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('chf') THEN 'CHF'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('£', 'gbp') THEN 'GBP'
            ELSE o.currencyisocode
        END AS currencyisocode,
        o.accountid
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }} o
)

SELECT 
    oc.id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(oc.name), ''), 'Unknown') AS "Name",
    COALESCE(oc.stagename, 'Prospecting') AS "StageName",
    oc.closedate AS "CloseDate",
    oc.amount AS "Amount",
    oc.currencyisocode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    a.id AS "AccountId",
    oc.id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM opportunity_cleaned oc
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a ON oc.accountid = a.id
```

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

    c.phone AS "Phone",
    c.title AS "Title",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.role__c)) IN ('decision maker', 'entscheider', 'end user', 'endanwender', 'technical contact', 'techniker', 'technischer ansprechpartner', 'executive sponsor', 'sponsor') THEN

                WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.role__c)) IN ('decision maker', 'entscheider') THEN 'Decision Maker'
                WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.role__c)) IN ('end user', 'endanwender') THEN 'End User'
                WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.role__c)) IN ('technical contact', 'techniker', 'technischer ansprechpartner') THEN 'Technical Contact'
                WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.role__c)) IN ('executive sponsor', 'sponsor') THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
            END
        WHEN c.role__c IS NULL OR TRIM(c.role__c) = '' THEN NULL
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.preferred_language__c)) IN ('de', 'deutsch', 'german', 'en', 'englisch', 'english', 'fr', 'französisch', 'french') THEN 
            CASE 
                WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.preferred_language__c)) IN ('de', 'deutsch', 'german') THEN 'DE'
                WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.preferred_language__c)) IN ('en', 'englisch', 'english') THEN 'EN'
                WHEN TRIM(LOWER(c.preferred_language__c)) IN ('fr', 'französisch', 'french') THEN 'FR'

        WHEN c.preferred_language__c IS NULL OR TRIM(c.preferred_language__c) = '' THEN NULL

    c.id AS "Legacy_Contact_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",

    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'contact') }} c
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a ON c.accountid = a.id

## Installed_Asset__c
```sql

WITH parsed_dates AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        warranty_end_date__c,
        CASE
            WHEN warranty_end_date__c IS NULL THEN NULL
            WHEN warranty_end_date__c = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
            WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN 
                TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'YYYY-MM-DD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN 
                TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN 
                TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN 
                TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ELSE NULL
        END AS parsed_warranty_end_date
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'installed_asset__c') }}
)

SELECT
    ia.id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(ia.name, 'Unknown') AS "Name",
    ia.serial_number__c AS "Serial_Number__c",
    pd.parsed_warranty_end_date AS "Warranty_End_Date__c",
    a.id AS "Account__c",
    p.id AS "Project__c",
    ia.id AS "Legacy_Asset_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

LEFT JOIN parsed_dates pd ON ia.id = pd.id
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a ON ia.account__c = a.id
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'project__c') }} p ON ia.project__c = p.id
```

## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opportunity_cleaned AS (
    SELECT 
        o.id,
        o.name,
        -- Normalize StageName to target enum
        CASE 
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('needs analysis') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('value proposition') THEN 'Value Proposition'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('perception analysis') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'won') THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS stagename,
        -- Parse CloseDate to ISO format
        CASE 
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN o.closedate
            ELSE NULL
        END AS closedate,
        -- Clean and cast Amount
        CASE 
            WHEN o.amount ~ '^[0-9\-]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN o.amount::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN o.amount ~ '[0-9]' THEN 
                CASE 
                    WHEN o.amount ~ ',' THEN 
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(
                            REGEXP_REPLACE(o.amount, '[^0-9\-,]', '', 'g'),
                            '(\d+),(\d+)', '\1.\2', 'g'
                        )::DOUBLE PRECISION
                    ELSE 
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(o.amount, '[^0-9\-.]', '', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
                END
            ELSE NULL
        END AS amount,
        -- Normalize CurrencyIsoCode
        CASE 
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('eur', 'euro') THEN 'EUR'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('usd', 'dollar') THEN 'USD'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('chf') THEN 'CHF'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('£', 'gbp') THEN 'GBP'
            ELSE o.currencyisocode
        END AS currencyisocode,
        o.accountid
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }} o

messages.raw:
ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning
- query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect both source and output data

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise (cite specific data issues found via queries)

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 21, 40, 54, 181223, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_messy_data_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_messy_data_v2_src"."account" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.account  (126 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `erp_number__c` text NULL
- `customer_tier__c` text NULL
- `region__c` text NULL

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

                TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN 
                TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ELSE NULL

)

## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opportunity_cleaned AS (
    SELECT 
        o.id,
        o.name,
        -- Normalize StageName to target enum
        CASE 
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('needs analysis') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('value proposition') THEN 'Value Proposition'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('perception analysis') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'won') THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS stagename,
        -- Parse CloseDate to ISO format
        CASE 
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN o.closedate
            ELSE NULL
        END AS closedate,
        -- Clean and cast Amount
        CASE 
            WHEN o.amount ~ '^[0-9\-]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN o.amount::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN o.amount ~ '[0-9]' THEN 
                CASE 
                    WHEN o.amount ~ ',' THEN 
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(
                            REGEXP_REPLACE(o.amount, '[^0-9\-,]', '', 'g'),
                            '(\d+),(\d+)', '\1.\2', 'g'
                        )::DOUBLE PRECISION
                    ELSE 
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(o.amount, '[^0-9\-.]', '', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
                END
            ELSE NULL
        END AS amount,
        -- Normalize CurrencyIsoCode
        CASE 
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('eur', 'euro') THEN 'EUR'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('usd', 'dollar') THEN 'USD'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('chf') THEN 'CHF'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('£', 'gbp') THEN 'GBP'
            ELSE o.currencyisocode
        END AS currencyisocode,
        o.accountid
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }} o
)

SELECT 
    oc.id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(oc.name), ''), 'Unknown') AS "Name",
    COALESCE(oc.stagename, 'Prospecting') AS "StageName",
    oc.closedate AS "CloseDate",
    oc.amount AS "Amount",
    oc.currencyisocode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    a.id AS "AccountId",
    oc.id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM opportunity_cleaned oc
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a ON oc.accountid = a.id
```

```sql

SELECT

    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.project_status__c)) IN ('active', 'aktiv') THEN 'Active'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.project_status__c)) IN ('completed', 'abgeschlossen') THEN 'Completed'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.project_status__c)) IN ('in planning', 'planung') THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.project_status__c)) IN ('on hold', 'angehalten') THEN 'On Hold'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.project_status__c)) IN ('cancelled', 'storniert') THEN 'Cancelled'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    CASE
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN p.go_live_date__c
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Go_Live_Date__c",
    a.id AS "Account__c",
    o.id AS "Opportunity__c",
    p.id AS "Legacy_Project_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM

LEFT JOIN

LEFT JOIN

messages.raw:
ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning
- query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect both source and output data

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise (cite specific data issues found via queries)

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 21, 40, 54, 181223, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_messy_data_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_messy_data_v2_src"."account" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.account  (126 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `erp_number__c` text NULL
- `customer_tier__c` text NULL
- `region__c` text NULL
- `industry` text NULL
- `website` text NULL
- `billingcity` text NULL
- `billingcountry` text NULL
- `legacy_customer_id__c` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.contact  (244 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `firstname` text NULL
- `lastname` text NULL
- `email` text NULL
- `phone` text NULL
- `title` text NULL
- `role__c` text NULL
- `preferred_language__c` text NULL
- `accountid` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.installed_asset__c  (480 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `serial_number__c` text NULL
- `warranty_end_date__c` text NULL
- `account__c` text NULL
- `project__c` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.opportunity  (303 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `stagename` text NULL
- `closedate` text NULL
- `amount` text NULL
- `currencyisocode` text NULL
- `accountid` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.project__c  (190 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `project_status__c` text NULL
- `go_live_date__c` text NULL
- `account__c` text NULL
- `opportunity__c` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.

- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opportunity_cleaned AS (
    SELECT 
        o.id,
        o.name,
        -- Normalize StageName to target enum
        CASE 
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('needs analysis') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('value proposition') THEN 'Value Proposition'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('perception analysis') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'won') THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS stagename,
        -- Parse CloseDate to ISO format
        CASE 
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN o.closedate
            ELSE NULL
        END AS closedate,
        -- Clean and cast Amount
        CASE 
            WHEN o.amount ~ '^[0-9\-]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN o.amount::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN o.amount ~ '[0-9]' THEN 
                CASE 
                    WHEN o.amount ~ ',' THEN 
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(
                            REGEXP_REPLACE(o.amount, '[^0-9\-,]', '', 'g'),
                            '(\d+),(\d+)', '\1.\2', 'g'
                        )::DOUBLE PRECISION
                    ELSE 
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(o.amount, '[^0-9\-.]', '', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
                END
            ELSE NULL
        END AS amount,
        -- Normalize CurrencyIsoCode
        CASE 
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('eur', 'euro') THEN 'EUR'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('usd', 'dollar') THEN 'USD'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('chf') THEN 'CHF'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('£', 'gbp') THEN 'GBP'
            ELSE o.currencyisocode
        END AS currencyisocode,
        o.accountid
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }} o
)

SELECT 
    oc.id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(oc.name), ''), 'Unknown') AS "Name",
    COALESCE(oc.stagename, 'Prospecting') AS "StageName",
    oc.closedate AS "CloseDate",
    oc.amount AS "Amount",
    oc.currencyisocode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    a.id AS "AccountId",
    oc.id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM opportunity_cleaned oc
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a ON oc.accountid = a.id
```

        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.project_status__c)) IN ('in planning', 'planung') THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.project_status__c)) IN ('on hold', 'angehalten') THEN 'On Hold'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.project_status__c)) IN ('cancelled', 'storniert') THEN 'Cancelled'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    CASE
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN p.go_live_date__c
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Go_Live_Date__c",
    a.id AS "Account__c",
    o.id AS "Opportunity__c",
    p.id AS "Legacy_Project_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'project__c') }} p
LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a ON p.account__c = a.id
LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }} o ON p.opportunity__c = o.id

messages.raw:
ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning
- query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect both source and output data

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise (cite specific data issues found via queries)

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 21, 40, 54, 181223, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_messy_data_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_messy_data_v2_src"."account" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.account  (126 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `erp_number__c` text NULL
- `customer_tier__c` text NULL
- `region__c` text NULL
- `industry` text NULL
- `website` text NULL
- `billingcity` text NULL
- `billingcountry` text NULL
- `legacy_customer_id__c` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.contact  (244 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `firstname` text NULL
- `lastname` text NULL
- `email` text NULL
- `phone` text NULL
- `title` text NULL
- `role__c` text NULL
- `preferred_language__c` text NULL
- `accountid` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.installed_asset__c  (480 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `serial_number__c` text NULL
- `warranty_end_date__c` text NULL
- `account__c` text NULL
- `project__c` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.opportunity  (303 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `stagename` text NULL
- `closedate` text NULL
- `amount` text NULL
- `currencyisocode` text NULL
- `accountid` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.project__c  (190 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `project_status__c` text NULL
- `go_live_date__c` text NULL
- `account__c` text NULL
- `opportunity__c` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.

- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opportunity_cleaned AS (
    SELECT 
        o.id,
        o.name,
        -- Normalize StageName to target enum
        CASE 
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('needs analysis') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('value proposition') THEN 'Value Proposition'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('perception analysis') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'won') THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS stagename,
        -- Parse CloseDate to ISO format
        CASE 
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN o.closedate
            ELSE NULL
        END AS closedate,
        -- Clean and cast Amount
        CASE 
            WHEN o.amount ~ '^[0-9\-]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN o.amount::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN o.amount ~ '[0-9]' THEN 
                CASE 
                    WHEN o.amount ~ ',' THEN 
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(
                            REGEXP_REPLACE(o.amount, '[^0-9\-,]', '', 'g'),
                            '(\d+),(\d+)', '\1.\2', 'g'
                        )::DOUBLE PRECISION
                    ELSE 
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(o.amount, '[^0-9\-.]', '', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
                END
            ELSE NULL
        END AS amount,
        -- Normalize CurrencyIsoCode
        CASE 
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('eur', 'euro') THEN 'EUR'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('usd', 'dollar') THEN 'USD'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('chf') THEN 'CHF'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('£', 'gbp') THEN 'GBP'
            ELSE o.currencyisocode
        END AS currencyisocode,
        o.accountid
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }} o
)

SELECT 
    oc.id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(oc.name), ''), 'Unknown') AS "Name",
    COALESCE(oc.stagename, 'Prospecting') AS "StageName",
    oc.closedate AS "CloseDate",
    oc.amount AS "Amount",
    oc.currencyisocode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    a.id AS "AccountId",
    oc.id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM opportunity_cleaned oc
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a ON oc.accountid = a.id
```

        WHEN p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Go_Live_Date__c",
    a.id AS "Account__c",
    o.id AS "Opportunity__c",
    p.id AS "Legacy_Project_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM

LEFT JOIN

LEFT JOIN

messages.raw:
ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning
- query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect both source and output data

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise (cite specific data issues found via queries)

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 21, 40, 54, 181223, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_messy_data_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_messy_data_v2_src"."account" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.account  (126 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `erp_number__c` text NULL
- `customer_tier__c` text NULL
- `region__c` text NULL
- `industry` text NULL
- `website` text NULL
- `billingcity` text NULL
- `billingcountry` text NULL
- `legacy_customer_id__c` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.contact  (244 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `firstname` text NULL
- `lastname` text NULL
- `email` text NULL
- `phone` text NULL
- `title` text NULL
- `role__c` text NULL

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

                TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN

            ELSE NULL

## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opportunity_cleaned AS (
    SELECT 
        o.id,
        o.name,
        -- Normalize StageName to target enum
        CASE 
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('needs analysis') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('value proposition') THEN 'Value Proposition'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('perception analysis') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'won') THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS stagename,
        -- Parse CloseDate to ISO format
        CASE 
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN o.closedate
            ELSE NULL
        END AS closedate,
        -- Clean and cast Amount
        CASE 
            WHEN o.amount ~ '^[0-9\-]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN o.amount::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN o.amount ~ '[0-9]' THEN 
                CASE 
                    WHEN o.amount ~ ',' THEN 
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(
                            REGEXP_REPLACE(o.amount, '[^0-9\-,]', '', 'g'),
                            '(\d+),(\d+)', '\1.\2', 'g'
                        )::DOUBLE PRECISION
                    ELSE 
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(o.amount, '[^0-9\-.]', '', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
                END
            ELSE NULL
        END AS amount,
        -- Normalize CurrencyIsoCode
        CASE 
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('eur', 'euro') THEN 'EUR'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('usd', 'dollar') THEN 'USD'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('chf') THEN 'CHF'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('£', 'gbp') THEN 'GBP'
            ELSE o.currencyisocode
        END AS currencyisocode,
        o.accountid
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }} o
)

SELECT 
    oc.id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(oc.name), ''), 'Unknown') AS "Name",
    COALESCE(oc.stagename, 'Prospecting') AS "StageName",
    oc.closedate AS "CloseDate",
    oc.amount AS "Amount",
    oc.currencyisocode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    a.id AS "AccountId",
    oc.id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM opportunity_cleaned oc
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a ON oc.accountid = a.id
```

```sql

    p.id AS "Id",

    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.project_status__c)) IN ('active', 'aktiv') THEN 'Active'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.project_status__c)) IN ('completed', 'abgeschlossen') THEN 'Completed'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.project_status__c)) IN ('in planning', 'planung') THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.project_status__c)) IN ('on hold', 'angehalten') THEN 'On Hold'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.project_status__c)) IN ('cancelled', 'storniert') THEN 'Cancelled'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    CASE
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN p.go_live_date__c
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN p.go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p.go_live_date__c, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Go_Live_Date__c",
    a.id AS "Account__c",
    o.id AS "Opportunity__c",
    p.id AS "Legacy_Project_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'project__c') }} p
LEFT JOIN
